revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chiyomaru Kofun]][[ja:千代丸古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Chiyomaru Kofun | native_name =千代丸古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Chiyomaru Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Chiyomaru Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chiyomaru Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.220278|N|131.543222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oita|qid=Q199609|1=Ōita (city)}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|13|13.0|N|131|32|35.6|E||qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Chiyomaru Kofun'''|千代丸古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Miyaen neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139714|title=千代丸古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Chiyomaru Kofun is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} approximately two kilometers west of the confluence of the Ōita River and its tributary, the Kaku River. It is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|||qq=}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 15 meters and a height of approximately four meters. It is estimated that it was built around the beginning of the 7th century. The single horizontal cave-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of huge {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stones opens to the south, with a total length of 8.9 meters. The burial chamber proper measures 3.6 by two meters, with a height of 2.6 meters. Some traces of [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]] paint remain. The passage is 4.8 meters long and 1.9 meters wide. Both the burial chamber and the corridor have a 1.2 meter-high hip stone placed at the bottom of the side wall, and on the back wall, a 50 cm-thick stone shelf protrudes horizontally about one meter above the platform which held the body. This tomb feature is found in Kyushu from southern {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} to northern {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. The front surface of the stone trellis is carved into a flat surface, with line engravings of triangles, people, and animals.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Kaku Station|en|Kaku Station|qid=Q6349670|lt=Kaku Station|1=Kaku Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|qid=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|1=Kyūdai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}} [[Decorated kofun]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o205/documents/tiyomarukohunn.pdf Oita city home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Chiyomaru Kofun]]
